steps.title: Distribute the negative sign through both terms
steps.content:
Apply the minus sign to each term in the second parentheses. Note that subtracting $−5$ becomes adding $+5$:

$$(3c + 9) - (4c^3 - 5) = 3c + 9 - 4c^3 + 5$$

steps.title: Combine constants and arrange by degree
steps.content:
Add the constant terms and write in standard form (highest degree first):

$$-4c^3 + 3c + (9 + 5) = -4c^3 + 3c + 14$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
